name: Iterative with Dummy Node
summary: Prepend a dummy node so the head can be removed uniformly; walk current through the list, skipping every node whose value equals val.
visualizationIntent: init: the full linked list is drawn with a dummy node prepended; current pointer sits on dummy. loop: the loop header fires — current.next is non-null so we enter the body. check: current.next.val is compared to val; the next node lights up as 'compared'. update (skip): if it matches, current.next is relinked to current.next.next — the matched node disappears from the chain. move (advance): if it does not match, current advances one step right. return: the dummy node is removed and the remaining list is shown as the result.

lineExplanations.3: Store the integer value on this ListNode.
lineExplanations.4: Set the next pointer to None (or the provided node) — None marks the tail.
lineExplanations.8: Convert the input array into actual ListNode objects so we have a real linked list to traverse.
lineExplanations.9: Build a Python list of ListNode objects, one per value in the input.
lineExplanations.10: Chain the nodes together by setting each node's .next to the following node.
lineExplanations.11: real_head is the first node — the true start of the list (or None if empty).
lineExplanations.12: Create a dummy node with value 0 placed before real_head. This sentinel means we never need special logic to handle removing the first node.
lineExplanations.13: Point dummy.next to real_head so dummy is the node just before the list.
lineExplanations.14: current starts at dummy — we will always inspect current.next rather than current itself.
lineExplanations.15: Build the nodes array for the visual: each entry has an id and a label showing the node's value.
lineExplanations.16: Build the links array for the visual: each entry represents a next-pointer arrow from node i to node i+1.
lineExplanations.17: changed_links tracks any newly added or rewritten arrows this step, so the animation can flash them.
lineExplanations.18: node_ids is a bookkeeping list of all node indices (used to track which nodes remain).
lineExplanations.19: id_map maps each ListNode's Python memory id to its visual id, so we can find node ids quickly.
lineExplanations.20: Give the dummy node a visual id of -1 (it is shown to the left of the main list).
lineExplanations.21: curr_id is the visual id of the current pointer's position; start it on dummy (-1).
lineExplanations.22: Keep looping as long as current.next exists — there is still a next node to examine.
lineExplanations.23: Record where current is in the visual so the pointer renders in the right place.
lineExplanations.24: next_id is the visual id of the node we are about to inspect.
lineExplanations.25: Check whether the next node's value equals val — this is the core decision point.
lineExplanations.26: Remember the id of the node we are about to skip.
lineExplanations.27: Find the id of the node after the one we are skipping (the one that will become current.next).
lineExplanations.28: Relink: current.next now skips over the matched node and points to the node after it.
lineExplanations.29: Remove the skipped node from both the links and nodes arrays so it disappears from the visual.
lineExplanations.30: Remove the skipped node from the nodes array too.
lineExplanations.31: Record the new arrow from current to the node after the skip so the animation can highlight it.
lineExplanations.32: Store the new relinked arrow in changed_links so the renderer flashes it (empty if after_id is None).
lineExplanations.33: The next node does not match val — advance current one step forward.
lineExplanations.34: Update curr_id to reflect that current has moved.
lineExplanations.35: Clear changed_links — no arrow was rewritten this step.
lineExplanations.36: Reset changed_links to empty — no link was rewritten because we only advanced, not deleted.
lineExplanations.37: Collect the values of the remaining nodes into result_vals by walking from dummy.next to the end.
lineExplanations.38: Start the result-collection walk at dummy.next (the new head).
lineExplanations.41: result holds the final ordered list of surviving values.
lineExplanations.42: Assign result_vals to result so the resultSpec variable can be found.
lineExplanations.43: Return result — the list of node values after all matched nodes have been removed.

syntaxExplanations.1: class ListNode: defines a singly-linked list node. In LeetCode this is already provided; we define it here so the tracer can run standalone.
syntaxExplanations.2: def __init__: the constructor. val stores the integer; nxt is the next node pointer (default None).
syntaxExplanations.3: self.val = val: store the integer value on this node.
syntaxExplanations.4: self.next = nxt: store the pointer to the next node (None means end of list).
syntaxExplanations.6: class Solution: the required LeetCode wrapper class.
syntaxExplanations.7: def removeElements(self, head, val): head is the first node (or []) and val is the integer to delete.
syntaxExplanations.8: vals = head if head else []: head arrives as a Python list from the preset; treat an empty list as [].
syntaxExplanations.9: [ListNode(v) for v in vals]: list comprehension — creates one ListNode for every integer in vals.
syntaxExplanations.10: for k in range(len(nodes_list) - 1): iterate over every node except the last to wire up .next pointers.
syntaxExplanations.11: nodes_list[k].next = nodes_list[k + 1]: connect node k to node k+1.
syntaxExplanations.12: real_head = nodes_list[0] if nodes_list else None: the first node is the head; None if the list is empty.
syntaxExplanations.13: dummy = ListNode(0): creates a sentinel node with value 0. It is never part of the answer — just a stable anchor.
syntaxExplanations.14: dummy.next = real_head: attach the real list to dummy.
syntaxExplanations.15: current = dummy: the traversal pointer starts at dummy so we can always look at current.next.
syntaxExplanations.16: nodes = [...]: build the visual nodes array as a list of dicts with 'id' and 'label'.
syntaxExplanations.17: links = [...]: build the visual links array as dicts with 'from' (source id) and 'to' (target id).
syntaxExplanations.18: changed_links = []: will hold any arrows that were just rewritten — starts empty.
syntaxExplanations.19: node_ids = list(range(...)): integer ids 0,1,2,... one per node.
syntaxExplanations.20: id_map = {id(nodes_list[i]): i ...}: Python's id() gives the memory address; we map it to our visual id.
syntaxExplanations.21: id_map[id(dummy)] = -1: assign the dummy node its special visual id of -1.
syntaxExplanations.22: curr_id = -1: visual id of where current sits right now (dummy).
syntaxExplanations.23: while current.next is not None: keep going as long as there is a node ahead to inspect.
syntaxExplanations.24: curr_id = id_map.get(id(current), -1): look up the visual id of the node current points to.
syntaxExplanations.25: next_id = id_map.get(id(current.next), -1): look up the visual id of the node current.next points to.
syntaxExplanations.26: if current.next.val == val: compare the NEXT node's value to the target — this is the delete check.
syntaxExplanations.27: skip_id = next_id: save the id of the node we are about to remove.
syntaxExplanations.28: after_id = ...: find the id of the node that will become the new current.next after deletion.
syntaxExplanations.29: current.next = current.next.next: the bypass — current now points two nodes ahead, cutting out the matched node.
syntaxExplanations.30: [lk for lk in links if lk['from'] != skip_id and lk['to'] != skip_id]: filter out all arrows connected to the deleted node.
syntaxExplanations.31: [nd for nd in nodes if nd['id'] != skip_id]: filter out the deleted node from the visual nodes list.
syntaxExplanations.32: changed_links = [...]: record the new arrow so the animation highlights it.
syntaxExplanations.33: else: the next node's value does NOT equal val — we keep it and move on.
syntaxExplanations.34: current = current.next: advance current by one node.
syntaxExplanations.35: curr_id = id_map.get(id(current), -1): update the visual id to reflect the new position.
syntaxExplanations.36: changed_links = []: no arrows changed this step.
syntaxExplanations.37: result_vals = []: start an empty list to collect surviving values.
syntaxExplanations.38: node = dummy.next: start the result walk at the new head (first node after dummy).
syntaxExplanations.39: result_vals.append(node.val): add this surviving node's value to the output.
syntaxExplanations.40: node = node.next: move to the next surviving node.
syntaxExplanations.41: result = result_vals: name the output 'result' so the resultSpec can find it.
syntaxExplanations.42: return result: hand back the list of surviving values as the answer.
